title: 8 Creating, Modifying, Deleting, and Receiving Trace Requests on OLT
body_text:
8	Creating, Modifying, Deleting, and Receiving Trace Requests on OLT
8.1	Overview of Processing Trace Requests on OLT
Both Tracers and SuperTracers can create and receive trace requests on OLT. After Central 1 receives a trace request from the financial institution, the trace request is forwarded to the appropriate biller for investigation.
The status of the trace request is updated on OLT as it is processed (e.g., when it is sent to the biller, when the biller responds). If an adjustment is required, settlement is forwarded to the financial institution when authorization for the adjustment is received from the biller.
OLT is also used to advise financial institutions of biller-initiated adjustments and trace requests. Biller-initiated requests for information arenГўВҖВҷt processed on OLT.
See Chapter 11, Creating Electronic Bill Payment Trace Requests Outside of OLT, if:
The transaction was created more than one year ago (the OLT database contains data for one year only).
The transaction canГўВҖВҷt be located on OLT. The Tracer or SuperTracer must first verify the transaction details, re-enter the search criteria on OLT, and confirm that the transaction was successfully remitted to CentralВ 1.
OLT is unavailable.
8.2	Creating Trace Requests on OLT
A trace can include a request for information (e.g., confirmation of the date the payment was applied), the provision of new information (e.g., the correct biller account number), or an adjustment request (e.g., the member paid twice). To create a trace request, a Tracer or SuperTracer selects the appropriate transaction from the OLT database. The transaction details, other than the memberГўВҖВҷs name, are automatically populated in the trace request. Transaction data is stored in the OLT database for one year.
Note: 	Central 1 doesnГўВҖВҷt process trace requests for transactions of less than $20.
A Tracer or SuperTracer proceeds as follows to create a trace request on OLT:
Log in to OLT, as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to OLT.
Select
Search Transaction
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Search Transaction
Enter search criteria to locate the transaction that you want to trace. See Figure 2. Proceed as follows:
Note:	The start date and amount are required information. At least one of the following fields must also be completed: trace number, vendor name, member account number, or member vendor account number.
ГўВҲВ’
Start Date
ГўВҖВ“ Select the calendar icon to select the transaction date.
ГўВҲВ’
End Date
ГўВҖВ“ If the exact date of the transaction is unknown, select the calendar icon to select an end date. The date range must not exceed seven days.
Note:	As the date range increases, the search response time decreases.
ГўВҲВ’
Amount ($)
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the transaction amount.
ГўВҲВ’
Enter at least one of the following:
ГўВҲВ’
Trace #
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the trace number of the original transaction.
ГўВҲВ’
Vendor Name
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the name of the biller. A list of possible matches appears after three or more characters are typed into this field. Select the appropriate biller from the list of matches.
ГўВҲВ’
Member Account #
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the memberГўВҖВҷs account number with your financial institution.
ГўВҲВ’
Member Vendor Account #
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the memberГўВҖВҷs account number with the biller.
Figure 2: Enter Search Criteria
Select
Search
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Search
Important
:	If you canГўВҖВҷt locate the transaction on OLT, verify the transaction details and re-enter the search criteria. If the transaction still doesnГўВҖВҷt appear on OLT, confirm that it was successfully remitted to Central 1. The transaction may have been rejected and returned to your financial institution. If the trace request canГўВҖВҷt be sent via OLT, submit it to Central 1 manually as described in Chapter 11, Creating Electronic Bill Payment Trace Requests Outside of OLT.
Select the transaction you want to trace from the search results.
If the transaction was previously traced or is currently under investigation, select
Open Trace Request
to view the trace request. See Figure 4.
Note:	A new trace request canГўВҖВҷt be created if a trace request for the same transaction is open. However, if the transaction was previously traced and the request was closed, a new trace request can be created. To avoid duplication, review the transaction details before creating a new trace request.
Figure 4: View Existing Trace
Select
Initiate a New Trace Request
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Initiate Trace Request
Complete the Reason for Trace section as follows. See Figure 6.
Tip
:	Mandatory fields are marked with a double asterisk ГўВҖВң**ГўВҖВқ on the reason for trace page.
Note:	When entering a dollar value, use a decimal to indicate cents. DonГўВҖВҷt use commas.
ГўВҲВ’
Credit Union Initiated Request
is selected by default.
ГўВҲВ’
Select the reason for the trace request and provide additional information as required. Select the button beside one of the following options:
ГўВҲВ’
MemberГўВҖВҷs account at vendor not credited
ГўВҲВ’
Payment made to an incorrect account number
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the correct account number.
ГўВҲВ’
Payment made to incorrect vendor
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the amount of the refund request.
ГўВҲВ’
Incorrect amount paid
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the amount of the transaction in the Paid field. Enter the amount that the member meant to pay in the Should Be field. The system will calculate the amount of the refund request.
ГўВҲВ’
Duplicate Payment
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the amount of the refund request.
ГўВҲВ’
A Fraudulent Transaction
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the amount of the refund request.
ГўВҲВ’
Other
ГўВҖВ“ Select this if your trace reason is not addressed by any of the other trace reason options. Enter in any relevant supporting information and the amount of the refund request.
Enter the memberГўВҖВҷs name, address, and phone number. The name is mandatory.
Note:	The transaction date, member account number, vendor name, member vendor account number, amount, and trace number are automatically populated.
If necessary, record additional information about the trace request in the Additional Comments field.
Select
Next
.
Figure 6: Trace Request Details
Review the trace request and select
Confirm and Submit Trace
.
If a Tracer created the trace request, ask a SuperTracer to authorize the trace request.
8.3	Authorizing Trace Requests on OLT
Trace requests created by Tracers require authorization (trace requests created by SuperTracers donГўВҖВҷt require authorization). Only SuperTracers can authorize trace requests.
Unauthorized trace requests are displayed in the OLT Inbox and are sent to Central 1 after they are authorized. Financial institutions are responsible for authorizing trace requests in a timely manner.
A SuperTracer proceeds as follows to authorize a trace request:
Log in to OLT as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to OLT.
Select the trace request that you want to authorize if it is displayed in the Inbox on the home page. See Figure 7.
Tip
:	Unauthorized trace requests are colour-coded green.
Note:	The ten most recent trace requests in the Inbox are displayed on the home page.
Figure 7: Select Trace Request
If the trace request that you want to authorize isnГўВҖВҷt displayed on the home page, proceed as follows.
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Inbox
or
click to view full list
to view all trace requests in the Inbox. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: View All Traces
ГўВҲВ’
Use the arrows in the Status column to sort the traces by status. All unauthorized traces will be grouped together. See Figure 9.
Note:	The sort option isnГўВҖВҷt available on the home page.
Figure 9: Sort by Status
ГўВҲВ’
Select the trace request that you want to authorize.
Review the trace request details and select
Approve and Send
. The status changes to ГўВҖВңnew-approvedГўВҖВқ and the trace request is moved to the Outbox. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Approve Trace Request
Select
Close
when prompted.
8.4	Modifying Trace Requests on OLT
SuperTracers can modify unauthorized trace requests. If it is necessary to modify a trace request that was sent to Central 1, the modifications must be made by Central 1 while the trace request is open. If the trace request is closed, create a new trace request. Biller-initiated trace requests canГўВҖВҷt be modified.
Modifying Unauthorized Trace Requests
A SuperTracer proceeds as follows to modify an unauthorized trace request:
Log in to OLT as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to OLT.
Select the unauthorized trace request that you want to modify if it is displayed in the Inbox on the home page. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Select Trace Request
If the unauthorized trace request that you want to modify isnГўВҖВҷt displayed on the home page, proceed as follows.
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Inbox
or
click to view full list
to view all trace requests in the Inbox. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: View All Traces
ГўВҲВ’
Use the arrows in the Status column to sort the traces by status. All unauthorized traces will be grouped together. See Figure 13.
Note:	The sort option isnГўВҖВҷt available on the home page.
Figure 13: Sort by Status
ГўВҲВ’
Select the trace request that you want to modify.
Select
Modify Trace Request
. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Modify Trace Request
Make the necessary changes (e.g., update the reason for the trace request) and then select
Next
. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Select Next
Select
Confirm
and
Submit Trace
. See Figure 16.
Figure 16: Confirm and Submit
Locate the modified trace request in the Inbox, open the details, and select
Approve and Send
. The trace request must be authorized before it is sent to Central 1. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Approve and Send
Select
Close
when prompted.
Modifying Trace Requests that Have Been Sent to Central 1
If the trace request that you want to modify was sent to Central 1, the modifications must be made by Central 1 while the trace request is open. Email the details of the trace request, the requested change, and the reason for the change to
bptrace@central1.com.
If the trace request is closed, create a new trace request.
8.5	Deleting Trace Requests on OLT
SuperTracers can delete unauthorized trace requests. To delete a trace request that has been sent to CentralВ 1 and is open, contact CentralВ 1. Closed trace requests and biller-initiated trace requests canГўВҖВҷt be deleted.
Deleting Unauthorized Trace Requests
A SuperTracer proceeds as follows to delete an unauthorized trace request:
Log in to OLT, as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to OLT.
Select the unauthorized trace request that you want to delete if it is displayed in the Inbox on the home page. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Select Trace Request
If the unauthorized trace request that you want to delete isnГўВҖВҷt displayed on the home page, proceed as follows:
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Inbox
or
click to view full list
to view all trace requests in the Inbox. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: View All Traces
ГўВҲВ’
Use the arrows in the Status column to sort the traces by status. All unauthorized traces will be grouped together. See Figure 20.
Note:	The sort option isnГўВҖВҷt available on the home page.
Figure 20: Sort by Status
ГўВҲВ’
Select the trace request that you want to delete.
Select
Delete/Reject Trace Request
. See Figure 21.
Figure 21: Delete Trace Request
Select
OK
to confirm.
Deleting Trace Requests Sent to Central 1
To delete a trace request that was sent to Central 1 and is still open, email the details of the trace request with instructions to delete it to
bptrace@central1.com.
If the trace request is closed, it canГўВҖВҷt be deleted.
8.6	Receiving Biller-Initiated Trace Requests on OLT
Central 1 will enter a trace request on OLT if it receives a biller-initiated notification (e.g., the biller provides the correct biller account number) or an adjustment regarding a memberГўВҖВҷs payment.
If the biller initiates a query and requests the name of the joint account holder or the memberГўВҖВҷs phone number or address, the financial institution must obtain authorization from the member to provide this information to the biller. The request wonГўВҖВҷt be processed on OLT. See the Electronic Bill Payments System Part for more information. If the member doesnГўВҖВҷt provide authorization, the biller will return the payment to the financial institution. In this case, Central 1 will enter a biller-initiated trace with an adjustment on OLT.
It is recommended that the financial institution check for biller-initiated trace requests on a daily basis. Tracers and SuperTracers can view biller trace requests for all branches of their financial institution.
Biller-initiated trace requests initially appear in the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs OLT Outbox. At the end of the business day the traces are closed and moved to the Inbox. To identify these traces, the financial institution must open the trace request details.
A Tracer or SuperTracer proceeds as follows to process biller-initiated trace requests:
Log in to OLT, as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to OLT.
Open the details of a trace request to determine if a biller created it. Select the closed traces in the Inbox and select the open traces in the Outbox. See Figure 22.
Note: 	Select
click to view full list
, to view all trace requests in the Inbox or Outbox.
Figure 22: Select Trace Request
If the ГўВҖВңReason for TraceГўВҖВқ section of the trace request details reads ГўВҖВңVendor InitiatedГўВҖВқ, review the trace and take any necessary action. The trace request details indicate if an adjustment is pending, the amount of the service charge (if applicable), the reason for the trace, and any new account information provided by the biller. See Figure 23.
Figure 23: Biller-Initiated Trace
Notify your member if the biller provides an updated account number.
END
